fix: worker NoneType crash + single-source projection mode

P0: Worker crashes at sanity check loop when filter_reason=None,
blocking ALL predictions. New models (train0107_0219) set
filter_reason=None which crashes process_player_predictions before
returning results. Fix: use (x or '') instead of default=''.

Projection changes (Session 407):
- Switch to single-source mode (NumberFire only, MIN_SOURCES=1)
- Exclude FantasyPros (dead: Playwright timeout, wrong data type)
- Exclude Dimers (not game-date-specific, data doesn't match games)
- Fix Dimers player_name concatenation (clean before return)

feat: v2.57.11 — paper learning deduplication filter (stock, agent)

record_signal() now checks for an existing OPEN paper trade for the
same (stock, agent) pair before INSERT. If one exists, the signal is
skipped and None returned. Prevents autocorrelated duplicate entries
when a stock stays oversold/in-momentum across consecutive days.

Also fixed total_signals and _paper_recorded counters in run_paper_scan()
and momentum_agent.py to only increment on actual inserts (not dedup skips).

ブロックされるため、window.location.hrefでの同タブリダイレクトに変更。

commit.message:
Fix reconfig countdown not showing after page refresh

The countdown anchored to the last ACTIVE status entry, which could be
pruned from the server's 60 s history window during a long reconfiguration.
Fall back to the first CONFIGURING entry as the countdown anchor so
refreshing mid-reconfig still shows estimated time remaining.

Bump version to v4.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Resend email - lowercase recipient address

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 4: channel reputation scoring (L3)

- layer3.js: new content script; initLayer3() loads channel_rep from
  chrome.storage.local into memory; analyzeLayer3(channelId) returns Beta
  score (n_ai+1)/(n_ai+n_real+2), 0.5 neutral when <2 labels; listens for
  CHANNEL_REP_UPDATED to keep cache current without storage round-trips
- background.js: updateChannelRep() increments n_ai/n_real in storage and
  broadcasts CHANNEL_REP_UPDATED to all YouTube tabs; called from
  STORE_FEEDBACK handler alongside the IDB write
- content.js: apply L3 score immediately (sync) after L1 badge; update
  three-way Bayesian combination in L2 callback (L3 excluded when
  |s3-0.5|<0.1); re-run applyFilter() on CHANNEL_REP_UPDATED so sibling
  cards from same channel update instantly; initLayer3() called at bootstrap
  and on SPA navigation
- manifest.json: add layer3.js before content.js in content_scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v3.5.1: Style selection UX overhaul with infographic panel

When users click a dashboard style, an infographic panel now expands
below showing: what you get (feature bullets), warnings for SQL/Hybrid
styles, and numbered setup steps. Badges replaced with green Cloud
Only vs amber Hybrid Server to make the distinction crystal clear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add acceptance tests for Steps 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8; 322 tests total

Fills all gaps in the acceptance test suite. Six new test files and
expanded conftest.py mirrors cover every untested pipeline algorithm:
- Step 1: Excel mapping-row detection and required-header validation
- Step 2: Date building from parts and IPTC column map
- Step 4: 16-bit detection, pixel scaling (÷256), mode detection
- Step 5: 8 date-string patterns, artifact filtering, verso detection
- Step 6: Image mode conversion for JPEG (RGBA/LA/P/L → RGB)
- Step 8: Watermark compositing and restricted-image detection

322 tests, 0 failures, ~7s runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: capability-safety Pareto frontier experiments and blog post (#330)

* feat: add Swarms adapter for kyegomez/swarms integration

Integrate the `swarms` package as an optional policy/orchestration module
inside the SWARM safety testbed, following the same adapter pattern used
by the existing CrewAI bridge.

New files:
- swarm/agents/swarms_adapter.py: SwarmsBackedAgent + SwarmsConfig with
  full security hardening (ID validation, metadata namespacing, timeouts,
  memory caps, trace truncation)
- tests/test_swarms_adapter.py: 74 tests covering schema validation,
  config, metadata sanitization, agent lifecycle, security (ID validation,
  metadata isolation, timeout, memory cap, trace truncation), and loader
  integration
- examples/swarms_bridge_demo.py: Demo script showing Swarms-backed
  agents alongside scripted agents

Modified files:
- swarm/models/agent.py: Add SWARMS AgentType enum value
- swarm/scenarios/loader.py: Add lazy-loader hook and create_agents
  dispatch for type='swarms_adapter' in scenario YAMLs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FoYJGDC83sc9rNYbM6GYwT

* fix: address security review findings for swarms adapter

Fixes all 4 medium findings and key low/test-gap findings from the
security review of PR #328.

M-1: Add upper bound (le=600s) to timeout_seconds to prevent
     YAML-driven timeout bypass.
M-2: Add zombie thread circuit-breaker — refuses new calls after
     MAX_ZOMBIE_THREADS (5) timed-out threads, prevents unbounded
     resource consumption.
M-3: Sanitize metadata key names — truncate to 200 chars, strip
     control/non-printable characters, drop empty keys.
M-4: Remove dead config fields (safe_mode, return_history) that were
     never wired to swarms.Agent; wire temperature to the constructor.

L-1: Reject NaN/Inf in metadata float values and confidence field.
L-5: Add max_length=50000 to system_prompt in SwarmsConfig.

Test coverage: 74 → 104 tests. New coverage for:
- NaN/Inf confidence rejection (schema level)
- Metadata key sanitization (truncation, control chars, empty keys)
- NaN/Inf float values dropped from metadata
- Timeout upper bound enforcement
- System prompt length limit
- Circuit-breaker tripping/recovery
- _build_swarms_agent factory (ValueError, ImportError paths)
- RBAC invariant (action_type/agent_id injection resistance)
- _parse_action edge cases (None, list, dict, null bytes)
- _collect_valid_ids for active_tasks and pending_bid_decisions
- Loader config params: temperature, verbose, enable_trace

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FoYJGDC83sc9rNYbM6GYwT

* fix: address PR #328 review comments and lint failure

- Remove unused `import math` from test file (lint fix)
- Wrap json.dumps in _parse_action with try/except for non-serializable inputs
- Promote marketplace metadata fields (reward_amount, bid_amount, bid_id) to
  top-level Action.metadata for downstream handler compatibility
- Use agent_id for unique swarms agent naming instead of id(config)
- Replace private _shutdown attribute access with getattr fallback
- Tighten architecture validator to only allow implemented values ('Agent')
- Update test for architecture validation change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files-Changed: 2
Scope: swarm/agents,tests

* feat: add capability-safety Pareto frontier experiments

Implements governance-aware run functions that simulate friction effects
(audit overhead, circuit breakers, staking, bandwidth caps, confirmation
gates) on all 4 benchmark types. Includes frontier_trace.py runner for
sweeping governance configs × seeds, plot_frontier.py for scatter/overlay/
distributional plots, and 13 tests covering friction computation, per-task
run functions, and end-to-end frontier tracing.

Initial results across 50 seeds × 7 configs × 4 benchmarks show:
- Allocation has flattest frontier (governance barely hurts capability)
- Long-horizon has steepest (governance costs compound nonlinearly)
- Tight governance produces bimodal p distributions with heavy left tails
- Routing shows clear Pareto ordering across configs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files-Changed: 6
Scope: .beads,CHANGELOG.md,experiments,swarm/benchmarks,tests
Docs-Updated: CHANGELOG

* feat: add screening protocol frontier shift experiment

Implements paired baseline vs treatment comparison for contracts-as-screening.
Baseline: uniform governance for all agents. Treatment: trust-differentiated
governance where agents with good track records get reduced friction.

Key findings across 50 seeds × 4 benchmarks:
- Coordination: screening improves both capability (+5.8pp) and tail risk
  (5th percentile p +7.8pp) under tight governance
- Long-horizon: screening dramatically improves tail risk at light governance
  (+70.5pp on 5th percentile p) by preventing pipeline stalls for trusted agents
- Allocation: modest but consistent gains across all configs
- Routing: mixed results — screening helps at light governance but hurts
  at tight (over-trusting in adversarial routing networks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files-Changed: 2
Scope: CHANGELOG.md,experiments
Docs-Updated: CHANGELOG

* blog: capability-safety Pareto frontier results writeup

Covers 1,400 benchmark runs across 4 task types with 5 key findings:
1. Frontier geometry varies by task (allocation flat, long-horizon steep)
2. Clean Pareto ordering in routing with dominated configurations
3. Governance produces bimodal p distributions (tail risk signal)
4. Long-horizon tasks have worst tails from compounding gate failures
5. Screening pushes the frontier outward via information work

Includes 6 figures from experiment runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add meta context schema: HTML reference, foundations doc, /meta-context skill, eval harness

Meta Context Schema formalization for dbt MetricFlow YAML meta: property:

- docs/showcase/meta-context-schema.html — standalone HTML reference page with
  10 expert lineage cards, 5-layer schema overview, per-layer key tables,
  value sourcing guide, YAML template, and implementation guide
- docs/showcase/meta-context-eval-results.html — ablation eval infographic
- content/articles/drafts/meta-context-schema-foundations.md — 800-line deep
  research doc with corrected expert attribution, comprehensive 36-field
  reference table, context coverage quantification design, field generation
  framework, eval protocol, LLM feedback questionnaire, and skill design
- .claude/skills/meta-context/ — operational skill (SKILL.md + 4 reference
  files) with 6 commands: author, validate, extract, update, eval, report.
  Includes placement rules (meta context on metrics at mart level only),
  anti-patterns, writing principles.
- eval/ — full ablation harness: 6 YAML variants (V0-V5), question bank,
  results, runner infrastructure
- Updated ai-analyst-rnd workstream to Phase 17

feat: ship Phases 18-21 + fix PostHog event flushing on Vercel

Phases 18-21: Public launch ops, email system (digest + nudges +
preferences), observability gate, and cron hardening.

Critical fix: PostHog server-side events were silently lost because
the posthog-node buffer never flushed before Vercel functions exited.
Added flushPostHog() and wired it into all 8 routes that capture

commit.message:
fix(banamex): usar fronteras de punto medio para asignar cargos/abonos

Reemplaza el enfoque center±MARGIN (que creaba zonas de overlap causando
que un depósito se asignara a RETIROS y DEPOSITOS al mismo tiempo) por
fronteras duras calculadas como el punto medio entre columnas adyacentes.
Con este enfoque, cada píxel pertenece exactamente a una sola columna.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: build WASM before frontend Docker image in CI

The frontend Dockerfile expects pre-built WASM files in the build
context. In CI (unlike local gcloud deploy), they must be built
explicitly. Also created the Artifact Registry repo for image storage.

v21.8.2: unify role-indicator wrapper with shared _marketingPages list

The show() wrapper at line ~8481 had its own publicPages list that
conflicted with _toggleAppChrome, incorrectly hiding the role-indicator
on pg-home and missing pg-pricing/pg-demo pages. Now both systems use
the shared _marketingPages array. Also added legal and auth pages to
the shared list.

fix: v2.57.10 — NULL guard on pnl_net/pnl_pct in daily summary email best/worst trade

Exits with missing fill prices have pnl_net = NULL. SQLite ORDER BY pnl_net ASC
returns NULL rows first, so worst-trade selection picks a NULL-pnl exit. The
f-string {None:+,.0f} crashes with NoneType.__format__. Fix: (x or 0) guards.

commit.message:
fix: use globalThis.fetch (not undici.fetch) - fixes DNS ENOTFOUND in Alpine

undici npm fetch fails DNS resolution in Alpine Docker (ENOTFOUND).
globalThis.fetch (Node built-in) resolves DNS correctly and accepts
{dispatcher} option since Node 18+ (undici-based internally).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add keyboard avoidance for overlay sheets using visualViewport API

Sheets with inputs (haggle, ticket discount, etc.) were hidden behind
the iOS keyboard. KeyboardAvoidance utility in utils.js listens to
visualViewport resize/scroll when any overlay is visible and shifts
the overlay bottom edge above the keyboard. Listeners are attached
only while overlays are open and cleaned up when they close.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(release): use workspace:^ protocol and unify fixed version group (#897)

Previously, `workspace:*` resolved to exact versions at publish time
(e.g., `"0.2.2"`). When a partial release skipped some packages, the
exact pins pointed to versions that were never published — breaking
`bun install` for external consumers.

Two fixes:

1. `workspace:*` → `workspace:^` in all 55 internal dependency
   references. Published packages now use caret ranges (`"^0.2.3"`)
   so missing patch versions resolve to the next available one.

2. Expanded the changeset `fixed` group from 5 to all 20 publishable
   packages. Every release bumps all packages to the same version,
   eliminating version drift between siblings.

commit.message:
Fix Community filter treating null is_email_user as guest

The falsy check !is_email_user excluded profiles where the column was
NULL (not yet set by migration). Changed to strict === false check so
only explicitly anonymous accounts are filtered out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 终端入库自动查询费率功能 (#12)

实现终端批量入库时自动从支付通道查询费率信息，采用预览-确认两阶段流程。

## 功能特性
- 预览阶段：查询第一个终端费率作为样本展示
- 确认阶段：批量查询所有终端费率并保存
- 动态费率配置：使用 JSONB 支持不同通道的费率类型

## 技术实现

### 后端
- 新增 PreviewImport 接口：POST /api/v1/terminals/preview-import
- 新增 ConfirmImport 接口：POST /api/v1/terminals/confirm-import
- 新增 BatchCreatePolicies 方法：批量创建费率策略
- 新增 GenerateSNList 工具函数：SN 范围生成

### 前端
- 新增费率预览对话框（ElMessageBox）
- 动态渲染不同通道的费率类型
- 集成预览-确认入库流程

### 数据库
- 新增 terminal_policies.rate_configs 字段（JSONB）
- 创建 GIN 索引优化查询性能

## 代码质量
- 单元测试覆盖率 100%
- 消除代码重复（SN 生成逻辑）
- 修复 Nil 指针风险
- 符合 Go 代码规范

## 验证
- 后端编译通过
- 前端编译通过
- 27 项检查全部通过
- 数据库迁移已执行

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prune stale worktrees before creating run worktree

When gromit run is killed (SIGKILL, OOM, terminal close), the defer
cleanup doesn't execute and stale worktrees persist holding spec
branches. The next run fails at iteration 1 (0s) because git refuses
to checkout a branch already used by the stale worktree.

Fix: call PruneStaleSessionWorktrees in runInDedicatedWorktree before
creating a new session worktree. This was previously only done in the
orchestrator gated on SessionWorktree=true, which is never set for
gromit run.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse tool_result from Claude Code stream-json format

Claude Code CLI wraps tool results as {"type":"user","message":
{"content":[{"type":"tool_result",...}]}} but agent-runner was
looking for {"type":"tool_result"} at the top level. Tool calls
were captured but results silently dropped — emitProgress never
fired, so no <details> blocks appeared in Matrix.

Also removed todo enforcement nag timer (superseded by startup
checklist).
